channel.title: Jeremydmiller.com on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Jeremydmiller.com on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Validation Options in Wolverine
item.description: Wolverine — the event-driven messaging and HTTP framework for .NET — provides a rich, layered set of options for validating incoming data. Whether you are building HTTP endpoints or message handlers, Wolverine meets you where you are: from zero-configuration inline checks to full Fluent Validation or Data Annotation middleware support for both command handlers and … Continue reading Validation Options in Wolverine → Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.com/2026/03/15/validation-options-in-wolverine/

item.title: New Improvements to Marten’s LINQ Support
item.description: I hold these two facts to be simultaneously true: Alright, on that note, let’s talk about a couple potentially important recent improvements to Marten’s LINQ support. First, we’ve received the message loud and clear that Marten was sometimes hard when what you really need is to fetch data from more than one document type at … Continue reading New Improvements to Marten’s LINQ Support → Link to article: https://jeremydmiller.com/2026/03/10/new-improvements-to-martens-linq-support/
